commit.message:
Add roxmltree to Cargo.lock (#368)

## Summary
- Add missing `roxmltree` dev-dependency to `Cargo.lock` (was added in
#367 but lockfile wasn't committed)
- Add `--locked` flag to all CI cargo commands (`clippy`, `doc`, `test`,
`build`) across `rust_tests.yml` and `newsletter.yml` to prevent
lockfile drift from going undetected

## Test plan
- [ ] CI passes with `--locked` flag on all cargo commands
- [ ] Deploy succeeds with updated lockfile

commit.message:
Harden chat recovery, Basecamp prompts, and workflow monitoring

Sprint 2A: Chat panel recovery polling extended from 60s to 120s
with auto-clear of stuck state. Sets chatStreamStartedAt on reconnect
so reconcileRunWithHistory can detect completed runs.

Sprint 3A: Strengthened Basecamp prompt discipline -- system prompt
now explicitly forbids memory-only answers for project data. Broadened
force-tool regex to catch "overdue", "blockers", "deadlines", "team",
"workload", and "status report" queries.

Sprint 3B: Enhanced workflow monitoring dashboard -- runs now show
workflow names instead of truncated IDs, added success rate metric,
failure callout when runs need attention, increased visible runs
from 5 to 8.

commit.message:
feat: add kafka trigger offset reset and auto.offset.reset config (#8283)

* fix: parallel branchall hang on bad stop_after_all_iters_if + results.x.length null

Two fixes:

1. When a parallel branchall/forloop has a `stop_after_all_iters_if` expression
   that fails (e.g. bad JS syntax), the error was propagated with `?`, causing
   the transaction to roll back the parallel index increment. Since all parallel
   jobs were already completed, nothing could ever increment the index again and
   the flow hung forever. Now the error is caught and converted to a stop-early
   failure so the transaction commits and the flow fails gracefully.

2. Expressions like `results.a.length` in step input transforms resolved to null
   because the `handle_full_regex` fast path intercepted them and used
   PostgreSQL's `#>` JSON path operator, which can't resolve JS runtime
   properties like `.length` on arrays. Now the fast path skips expressions
   ending with JS-only properties (like `length`), falling through to full
   QuickJS evaluation where they work correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add kafka trigger offset reset and auto.offset.reset configuration

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

execution/state: fix WriteAccountStorage skip using stale blockOriginStorage (#19748)

## Problem

\`applyStorageChanges\` passed \`blockOriginStorage[key]\` (the slot
value at **block start**) as the \`original\` parameter to
\`WriteAccountStorage\`. Both serial (\`Writer\`) and parallel
(\`BufferedWriter\`) implementations skip the write when \`original ==
value\`, to avoid redundant MDBX/pe.rs.accounts updates.

This skip fires **incorrectly** when a slot reverts to its block-start
value mid-block:
- tx_0 writes slot S = V_new → MDBX now holds V_new
- tx_1 writes slot S = V_prev (the block-start value) → skip fires →
MDBX still holds V_new

Subsequent blocks classify SSTORE incorrectly (SSTORE_SET vs
SSTORE_RESET), producing a gas mismatch with a diff that is a **multiple
of 17,100 gas**.

## Fix

\`applyStorageChanges\` is called from two paths with different
semantics:

- **\`FinalizeTx\`** (per-tx txNum): must use \`originStorage[key]\` —
the last value written to MDBX within this block — to avoid the A→B→A
false-skip bug.
- **\`CommitBlock\`/\`MakeWriteSet\`** (system txNum): must use
\`blockOriginStorage[key]\`. After all \`FinalizeTx\` calls,
\`originStorage[key]\` equals the final dirty value, so using it would
skip every write. The write at \`system_txNum\` is required by
\`ComputeCommitment\`.

Added \`useBlockOrigin bool\` parameter through \`updateAccount\` →
\`updateStorage\` → \`applyStorageChanges\`:
- \`FinalizeTx\` passes \`false\` → uses \`originStorage\` (fixes A→B→A
stale MDBX)
- \`MakeWriteSet\` passes \`true\` → uses \`blockOriginStorage\`
(preserves system-txNum write)

## Test plan

- [x] \`make erigon\` builds cleanly
- [x] \`go test ./execution/state/...\` passes (TestCreate2Revive,
TestAccountDeployIncorrectRoot, TestCustomTraceReceiptDomain)
- [x] CI green

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

commit.message:
feat(deploy): add full deploy script for Pi audio workstation (TK-059)

Explicit file-by-file deployment of all version-controlled configs and
scripts to the Pi. Covers PipeWire, WirePlumber, CamillaDSP, labwc,
systemd, Mixxx, wayvnc, and all test/stability scripts.

Features:
- --pi HOST: configurable SSH target
- --mode dj|live: sets CamillaDSP active config symlink
- --dry-run: prints full manifest without executing
- --reboot: optional post-deploy reboot
- D-023 enforcement: refuses to deploy uncommitted state
- wayvnc conditional deploy (never overwrites real password)
- Post-deploy verification: CamillaDSP syntax, start-mixxx syntax,
  libjack resolution warning
- Supersedes scripts/stability/deploy-to-pi.sh

Approved-by: Architect (full review, all 8 sections verified)

commit.message:
Instagram-level speed, quality, check-in flow & video support (#30)

* Fix 6 contract failures: double-wrap and partial user selects

Found via new API contract test script that validates every endpoint
returns the exact JSON shape iOS expects.

Fixes:
- checkins controller: return result.data array (not {data, total})
- restaurants checkins controller: same fix
- comments author hydration: include isPrivate, followerCount,
  followingCount, postCount in select (iOS User model requires them)
- notifications actor hydration: same expanded select
- Add scripts/api-contract-test.ts that logs in, hits every endpoint,
  and validates response shapes match iOS Codable models

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Exclude scripts/ from nest build to fix deploy crash

The api-contract-test.ts in scripts/ was being included in the nest
build, causing compilation failure and missing dist/main.js in prod.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Instagram-level speed, quality, check-in flow & video support

Performance:
- Add Nuke image caching (100MB memory + 300MB disk) with LazyImage
- Add blurhash placeholders for colored loading states
- Enable gzip compression for all API responses
- Add missing DB indexes (post.authorId, notification compound, follow reverse)
- Bump image quality: medium 1080px/q85, thumb 600px/q80, original 2048px/q92
- Add image prefetching for next 5 posts in feed scroll

Check-in flow:
- Restaurant selection is now mandatory for every post
- Rename "New Post" to "Check In" throughout (tab, nav title, buttons)
- Auto-create check-in record linked to post on creation

Video support (10s Reels-style):
- Backend: FFmpeg video processing (transcode to H.264 1080p, extract thumbnail)
- Backend: Accept video MIME types (mp4, quicktime, mov) up to 50MB
- iOS: Video selection from PhotosPicker with auto-thumbnail generation
- iOS: Auto-play muted video in feed with loop and mute toggle
- iOS: HEIC upload support (0.92 quality, fallback JPEG)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add FFmpeg to Dockerfile for video processing in production

The @ffmpeg-installer/ffmpeg npm package may not work on Alpine's musl libc,
so install system ffmpeg via apk as a reliable fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(analysis): add RSI and hidden divergence detection

Computes RSI(14) via ta library. Detects hidden bullish (price HL +
RSI LL) and bearish (price LH + RSI HH) divergences using

for chart line drawing.

Fix share links: clean ?loaded=true from URL after page load

When sharing from Safari, the URL was including ?loaded=true which
prevented the redirect to backend for OG meta tags. Now the URL
is cleaned up after load so shared links always go through the
backend OG redirect system.

probe (D-026) with retry loop before launching Mixxx via pw-jack. Fixes
F-022 (bare Mixxx launch without pw-jack wrapper).

TK-061: scripts/deploy/configure-libjack-alternatives.sh — Registers
PipeWire's libjack at higher priority than JACK2 via update-alternatives,
so bare mixxx uses PipeWire automatically. Addresses F-021 root cause.
